batch_metadata.note: August 2024 triage hot period. Aug 4-5 mega-cluster (COMP-RF04-016) is the primary flooding candidate for this hot period.

flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-6279 (Received): 'I'll contempt your sss' | ROW-6280 (Received): 'Ass' [rapid pair opener, then continued cascade across ~80 messages through Aug 5 night]
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: Aug 4-5 sustained mega-cluster — ~80 messages spanning Aug 4 evening through Aug 5 night. Triage hot period for RF-07 is August 2024, anchored in this cluster. ROW-6279-6280 open with two rapid consecutive messages ('I'll contempt your sss' / 'Ass'), followed by ROW-6287, 6290, 6296, 6297, 6298 — five additional Received messages addressing the same inadequate-father theme. Without individual message-level timestamps in the RF-04 batch record, the 5+/10-minute threshold cannot be confirmed from this source. However: (1) Aug 2024 is a confirmed RF-07 triage hot period, establishing this cluster as the primary hot-period anchor; (2) the cluster spans ~80 messages over two days with confirmed rapid-pair openers; (3) the ADD-001 threshold is met if any 10-minute window contains the ROW-6279-6298 sequence, which the rapid-pair opener (ROW-6279-6280, sent consecutively) suggests is likely. Classification: Sev-2 (dense cluster in confirmed hot period; flooding threshold probable but pending timestamp confirmation from raw data at synthesis). Compound with COMP-RF04-016 (RF-04+RF-05). ARC-RF07-001-S001.
verbatim_key_messages: ROW-6287 (Received): 'I can't understand why you have shared custody since you do nothing for her'
ROW-6290 (Received): 'Your answers are good evidence to show the court as to why you shouldn't have custody'
ROW-6296 (Received): 'She is also your daughter even though you don't act like it'
ROW-6297 (Received): 'You don't act like a father at all'
flagged_messages.calibration_notes: Timestamp confirmation required from raw Excel data at synthesis. Sev-2 assigned based on hot-period anchor designation and probable flooding given rapid-pair opener and ~80-message density.

unflagged_notable.message_text: ROW-6582: 'I don't want to hear about me being off work cause frankly my use of time is not your business but secondly you never take off work for her' | ROW-6583: 'If I don't take off she has no one to care for her' | ROW-6585: 'I don't have the opportunity to be an absent parent and just be like no I'm not doing anything. You do it'
unflagged_notable.assessment: Aug 8 re-opening of Aug 4-5 dispute — 4 messages, 4 days after the mega-cluster. RF-07 near-boundary: Melissa reopening a closed episode (continuation of prior conflict); however, 4 days post-closure exceeds the 'continuation-after-stop' window. This is more re-initiation than continuation. Not RF-07 primary. RF-04 peripheral (absent-parent framing continuation).

arcs_promoted_this_batch: ARC-RF07-001 promoted from watching to ACTIVE
arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF07-001 (Communication Boundary Violation Pattern) promoted to ACTIVE. S-001 is the first primary RF-07 signal in the dataset, anchored in the Aug 2024 hot period. Arc was in watching state from B-002 (Phase A patterns insufficient for promotion but consistent with eventual arc). Arc sub-patterns now tracking: flooding (primary in this batch), continuation-after-stop, response-demand, custodial-time-overstepping.

compound_patterns.summary: RF-04+RF-05+RF-07 triple compound. RF-04 (Sev-2: sustained inadequate-father mega-cluster; ARC-RF04-001-S46; 'shit father' new slur; financial false accusation; DARVO resolution) + RF-05 (contempt legal threats: 'contempt your ass' ROW-6279-6280; 'show the court' ROW-6290; 'file for sole custody' ROW-6312; ARC-RF05-001-S007) + RF-07 (Sev-2: ~80-message dense cluster in Aug 2024 hot period; flooding candidate; ARC-RF07-001-S001). Triggered by scheduling dispute over morning childcare; escalated into comprehensive character attack + parallel legal and flooding mechanisms.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: Mixed — predominantly cooperative Jul 16-Aug 3 (Canada trip return, illness logistics, birthday context), then two RF-04 clusters Aug 2 and Aug 4-5. The Aug 4-5 mega-cluster is the batch's defining event: ~80 messages spanning two days triggered by a scheduling dispute that escalated into sustained inadequate-father, financial, and legal-threat content. From RF-07 perspective, this is the Aug 2024 hot-period anchor.
batch_observations.rf07_assessment: B-017 contains the primary RF-07 hot-period event for Aug 2024. The mega-cluster density (~80 messages, Aug 4-5) is the flooding candidate. Phase A context: this batch falls in the transition from Phase A baseline toward Phase B escalation; the mega-cluster pattern here is early evidence of the communication-flooding dynamic that intensifies in Phase B.
batch_observations.custodial_context: Aug 4-5 dispute begins as a scheduling dispute over morning childcare — Eli was watching Olivia but work meetings arose. The dispute escalates while Eli is in his parenting role; Melissa's sustained messaging during this period constitutes contact into Eli's parenting time. Combined with ADD-004 confirmed sub-pattern.
